purpose: Read and mutate the account-to-fund assignment map. Used by the Accounts tab on the Fund TB Setup screen.

request_body_shape_assign.accountId: array<int> (one or more TB account ids - same id you see in the TB row)

rows.fund: object (embedded) OR ABSENT for unassigned accounts

response_shape_get.unassigned_signature: The `fund` key is OMITTED for unassigned accounts (not set to null). Use scripts.funds.split_assigned() to partition the DTOs.

rows.fund: object (embedded fund subset) OR ABSENT for unassigned accounts

response_shape_get.unassigned_signature: The `fund` key is OMITTED for unassigned accounts (not set to null). Use scripts.funds.split_assigned() to partition the DTOs.

semantics.incremental_not_whole_list: Unlike the FundType / Fund / FundSubType PUTs, this one is INCREMENTAL. Only the accounts in account_ids are affected; other assignments stay put.
semantics.unassign_signature: Omitting the fundId key entirely is what tells the server 'send these accounts back to Unassigned'. A null fundId is NOT equivalent - the field must be absent.
semantics.batch_assign: accountId is an array, so multiple accounts can be assigned to one fund in a single call. Different funds still need separate calls.

verification: After mutate, GET /v1/Fund/{clientId}/fundaccountmap and confirm the affected accountId rows have the expected fundId (or are missing if unassigned).
